response:
Emily collected 24 blueberries which is a solid number to start with. David collected 15 more than Emily so his total was 39 blueberries. This means David gathered more than Emily but not by an overwhelming margin. His collection was also three times what Sophia had which gives us a way to figure out how many blueberries Sophia collected.

***

Sophia collected 13 blueberries because 39 divided by 3 equals 13. This makes sense as it fits the condition that David had three times as many as Sophia. Adding up all the blueberries gives us the total number they had to work with. Emily had 24 David had 39 and Sophia had 13 making a total of 76 blueberries.

***

They used the blueberries to make muffins and each batch required 9 blueberries. Dividing 76 by 9 gives us 8 full batches with 4 blueberries remaining. These 8 batches were sold for $5 each which means they earned 40 dollars from their efforts. The leftover blueberries were not enough to make another batch so they were not used in the sale.

***

The money they made was directly tied to the number of batches they could produce. Each batch was sold for the same price and they made a total of 40 dollars. The process involved careful counting and division to ensure they maximized their resources. The experience taught them the value of teamwork and calculation in youth.
